questions.question: On the basis of chemical composition, proteins are divided into how many types?
questions.explanation: Page 422: Proteins are divided into three types: Simple Protein, Conjugated Protein, and Derived Protein.

questions.question: Simple Protein consists of only:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Simple Protein consists of only amino acids. Examples: Albumins, Globulins, Histones.

questions.question: Which is an example of Conjugated Protein?

options.C: Haemoglobin (having some other chemical compound in addition to amino acids)

questions.explanation: Page 422: Conjugated Protein has some other chemical compound in addition to amino acids, e.g. Haemoglobin.

questions.question: Derived Protein is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Derived Protein is derived from the partial hydrolysis of natural protein. Examples: Peptone, Peptide, Proteases.

questions.question: Animal protein is called first class protein because:

options.B: It contains all the essential amino acids

questions.explanation: Page 422: Animal protein is called first class protein because it contains all the essential amino acids.

questions.question: Egg contains how many essential amino acids?
questions.explanation: Page 422: Egg contains all the essential amino acids.

questions.question: Proteins are important for:

options.B: Physical growth, protoplasm and formation of cells, tissues

questions.explanation: Page 422: Proteins are important for physical growth, protoplasm and formation of cells, tissues.

questions.question: Lack of proteins hampers growth. This causes a disease called:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Lack of proteins causes Kwashiorkor and Marasmus in children.

questions.question: In Kwashiorkor disease, which parts of children are affected?

options.B: Stomach comes out, hands and legs get slimmed

questions.explanation: Page 422: In Kwashiorkor, the stomach comes out, and hands and legs of children get slimmed. Caused by protein deficiency.

questions.question: Marasmus is caused by:

options.B: Loosened deficiency of protein and energy

questions.explanation: Page 422: Marasmus is caused by loosened deficiency of protein and energy together.

questions.question: Biological catalysts which speed up chemical reactions and remain unchanged at 37°C are called:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Biological catalysts (enzymes) speed up chemical reactions and remain unchanged after reaction at 37°C.

questions.question: Human nails are made up of which protein?
questions.explanation: Page 422: Human nails are made up of protein Keratin.

questions.question: Soybean is the richest source of:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Soybean is the richest source of protein.

questions.question: When egg is heated, the transparent liquid portion turns solid. This happens due to:
questions.explanation: Page 422: When egg is heated, the protein turns solid due to thermal denaturation of protein (denaturation around at normal solution).

questions.question: Keratin is a protein which protects:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Keratin is the protein which protects the epithelial cells from damage.

questions.question: Fats are esters of:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Fats are esters of glycerol and fatty acid. They contain carbon, hydrogen and oxygen but proportionally less oxygen.

questions.question: Fats are of how many types?
questions.explanation: Page 422: Only two types – Saturated fatty acids (solid at 20°C, e.g. this is called fat) and Unsaturated fatty acids (liquid form, called oil).

questions.question: Saturated fatty acids are found in:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Saturated fatty acids are solid at 20°C temperature, this is called fat.

questions.question: Unsaturated fatty acids are found in:

options.B: Fish oil and vegetable oil (liquid form - called oil)

questions.explanation: Page 422: Unsaturated fatty acids are found in fish oil and vegetable oil. Examples: coconut oil, palm oil.

questions.question: An adult person should get approximately what percentage of energy from fat?
questions.explanation: Page 422: Normally an adult person should get approximately 20-30% of energy from fat.

questions.question: 1 gram of fat liberates how many kilo calories of energy?
questions.explanation: Page 422: 9.3 kilo calorie energy is liberated from 1 gram of fat.

questions.question: Fat stores maximum energy per:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Fat stores maximum energy per gram.

questions.question: Fat provides energy to the body and:

options.B: Prevents the loss of heat and remains under the skin

questions.explanation: Page 422: Fat provides energy to the body and prevents the loss of heat, remaining under the skin.

questions.question: Rancidity is the oxidation of fat. It changes the:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Rancidity takes place due to oxidation of fat and oils. It changes the taste and smell of food.

questions.question: Due to lack of fat, skin gets:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Due to the lack of fat, skin gets fatty/dry, the body weight of the body gets checked/decreases and development of the body is affected.

questions.question: Vitamin was first coined/invented by:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin was coined/invented by Sir F.G. Hopkins.

questions.question: The term 'Vitamine' was first used by:
questions.explanation: Page 422: The term vitamin was coined by Funk.

questions.question: On the basis of solubility, vitamins are of how many types?
questions.explanation: Page 422: On the basis of solubility, vitamins are of two types: (a) Vitamin soluble in water: Vitamin-B and Vitamin-C, (b) Vitamin soluble in fat: Vitamin-A, Vitamin-D, Vitamin-E and Vitamin-K.

questions.question: Which vitamins are water soluble?
questions.explanation: Page 422: Water soluble vitamins are Vitamin B and Vitamin C.

questions.question: Which vitamins are fat soluble?
questions.explanation: Page 422: Fat soluble vitamins are Vitamin A, D, E and K.

questions.question: Chemical name of Vitamin A is:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin A chemical name is Retinol.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin A causes:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin A deficiency causes night blindness and xerophthalmia.

questions.question: Chemical name of Vitamin B1 is:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B1 chemical name is Thiamine.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin B2 (Riboflavin) causes:

options.B: Cracking of skin, reddish eye, cracking at corners of lips, mouth ulcers

questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B2 deficiency causes cracking of skin, reddish eye, cracking at corners of lips, mouth ulcers.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin B3 (Nicotinamide/Niacin) causes:

options.B: Whitening of hair, mental retardation, Pellagra

questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B3 deficiency causes whitening of hair, mental retardation, Pellagra.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin B5 (Pantothenic acid) causes:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B5 (Pantothenic acid) deficiency causes burning sensation, dermatitis.

questions.question: Chemical name of Vitamin B6 is:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B6 chemical name is Pyridoxine.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin B6 causes:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B6 deficiency causes Anemia, skin disease, Depression.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin B7 (Biotin) causes:

options.B: Paralysis, body pain, hair falling, scaly and itchy skin

questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B7 (Biotin) deficiency causes paralysis, body pain, hair falling, scaly and itchy skin.

questions.question: Chemical name of Vitamin B9 is:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B9 chemical name is Folic acid.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin B9 (Folic acid) causes:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B9 deficiency causes Pernicious Anemia, jaundice.

questions.question: Chemical name of Vitamin B12 is:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B12 chemical name is Cyanocobalamin (or Cobalamin).

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin B12 causes:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B12 deficiency causes Anemia, dysentery.

questions.question: Chemical name of Vitamin C is:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin C chemical name is Ascorbic acid.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin C causes:

options.B: Scurvy, swelling of gums or bleeding gums

questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin C deficiency causes Scurvy, swelling of gums or bleeding gums.

questions.question: Sources of Vitamin A include:

options.B: Milk, Egg, Cheese, Green vegetable, Carrot

questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin A sources include Milk, Egg, Cheese, Green vegetables, Carrot.

questions.question: Kwashiorkor disease typically develops in children due to:

options.B: Protein deficiency while caloric intake may be adequate

questions.explanation: Page 422: Kwashiorkor is caused by protein deficiency, especially when caloric intake is not severely affected.

questions.question: Proteins take part in formation of:
questions.explanation: Page 422: Proteins take part in formation of cells, protoplasm and tissues.

questions.question: Sources of Vitamin B1 (Thiamine) include:

options.B: Pulse, Liver, Egg, Milk, eggs etc.

questions.explanation: Page 422: Sources of Vitamin B1 include Pulse, Liver, Egg, Milk, eggs.

questions.question: Vitamin B1 (Thiamine) deficiency causes which disease?
questions.explanation: Page 422: Vitamin B1 (Thiamine) deficiency causes Beriberi.
